answer:
likely $0 federal 🎉

why: shares acquired 2016 = post-9/27/2010 QSBS → 100% gain exclusion under §1202, up to greater of $10M or 10x basis per issuer. ur $8M gain fits under $10M cap → fully excluded. excluded gain also exempt from AMT + 3.8% NIIT

caveats that matter:
- must be original issuance direct from co (not bought secondhand), co was domestic C-corp w/ <$50M gross assets at issuance, active qualified trade/biz (no law/finance/consulting/hospitality etc). u said QSBS-qualified so assuming these check out
- 5-yr hold easily met (10 yrs)
- if u already used part of ur $10M cap on same issuer b4, remaining excess taxed at 28% QSBS rate + NIIT
- state tax separate — CA, PA, few others don't honor exclusion, so could owe state

2025 tax law changes (higher $15M cap, tiered holding) only apply to stock acquired after 7/4/25, don't affect u

rec: get CPA to paper the QSBS qualification (co rep letter, cap table proof) b4 sale — IRS scrutiny is real on $8M exclusions
